The Iraqi people's national demand from Al-Sudani's government: Take back Iraq's skies from America ▪️ Exclusive report by Tasnim correspondent in Baghdad (via Tasnim News ) 🔴 The Iraqi people believe their country's skies have been turned into an open corridor for American and Israeli warplanes and drones. These aircraft not only carry out attacks on Iran but also bomb the centers and headquarters of the Islamic Resistance in Iraq , particularly the Popular Mobilization Forces ( Al-Hashd Al-Shaabi ). ➡️ Sabah Zanganeh, head of the Iraqi Center for Strategic Studies , said: "This issue directly undermines Iraq's sovereignty and territorial integrity, and clearly shows that the Iraqi government's authority over its own airspace has been weakened." ➡️ What stings most for every Iraqi citizen is the feeling that, in the face of this violation of their nation's sovereignty, they can do nothing but condemn it and express their disgust. ➡️ That may explain why, in recent weeks, pressure has mounted on Iraqi officials—especially Prime Minister Mohammed Shia' Al-Sudani —to take concrete measures to end this situation. ➡️ At the same time, the Iraqi Parliament has called for revising the current security agreement between Baghdad and Washington and has urged the purchase of powerful, effective air defense systems from other countries, such as Russia , China , and South Korea . 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇺🇸 Houston, We Have a Smell Now halfway to the Moon, the Artemis II crew has reported their second toilet issue of the mission. Astronauts say there is a burning, heater-like smell coming from the toilet, though mission control is not concerned. The first problem with the $23 million Orion space toilet was reported just hours after launch, when a fan was found to be jammed. The crew was able to fix the issue with guidance from mission control. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

⚡️ 🇧🇭 🇵🇰 Bahrain’s Foreign Minister Dr. Abdullatif Al-Zayani held a phone call with Pakistan’s Foreign Minister Muhammad Ishaq Dar. They discussed ways to strengthen bilateral cooperation and coordinate on regional developments amid continued Iranian missile and drone attacks on Bahrain and GCC countries. Both stressed the importance of joint diplomatic efforts in international forums, including their current non-permanent seats on the UN Security Council. @wfwitness

🇫🇷 🗣 Politico: France intends to increase its stockpiles of missiles and drones by four times by 2030 The newspaper notes that next week the French government will present an updated law on military planning, which provides for the allocation of €8.5 billion for drones and missiles until 2030. The document represents a multi-year financial framework that defines the goals of arms procurement and development. France plans to increase its stockpiles of cruise missiles, including "kamikaze drones", by 400%, guided bombs AASM Hammer produced by Safran - by 240%, and Aster and Mica missiles - by 30%. Also, the publication reports that Paris is considering the development of a new tank, which is supposed to replace the Leclerc tank. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
